name: CALCIFIC LONGUS COLLI TENDINITIS
definition: ACUTE CALCIFIC RETROPHARYNGEAL TENDINITIS calcification anterior to C1-C2 ← deposition of calcium hydroxyapatite

demographics.Location: near insertion of longus colli tendon in close proximity to anterior arch of C1

distribution: near insertion of longus colli tendon in close proximity to anterior arch of C1
discriminating_features: amorphous calcification anterior to C1-C2 (CT most sensitive)
fluid originating in prevertebral space ± extension to retropharyngeal space:
retropharyngeal space smoothly expanded in all directions from C1 tapering inferiorly to
effusions may involve both lateral atlantoaxial joints

general: amorphous calcification anterior to C1-C2 (CT most sensitive)
fluid originating in prevertebral space ± extension to retropharyngeal space:
retropharyngeal space smoothly expanded in all directions from C1 tapering inferiorly to
effusions may involve both lateral atlantoaxial joints
